name: Protein Sugar Cookies (Caramel + Graham)
description: Protein sugar cookie reworked into a salted-caramel + graham cookie, bakery-style: big graham cracker shards pressed onto the TOP before baking so they anchor, toast, and stay crisp (not crumbs folded into the dough, which go soft), then a sugar-free caramel drizzle finished with a pinch of flaky salt and one fresh graham shard set into the caramel. Salt is the contrast that keeps caramel+graham from reading one-note sweet. First draft to tune — caramel is the lean SF syrup (0 cal) by default; swap in the real brown-sugar caramel for a richer read at a small calorie cost. Computed batch macros ~513 kcal / 41P / 56C / 13F (128/cookie at 4; ratio 12.6).

payload.name: honey graham crackers (broken into big shards)

payload.instructionText: Once the cookies are set and cooled, drizzle with caramel, then add a small pinch of flaky salt over the drizzle — salted caramel, the contrast that keeps it from being one-note sweet. Press one reserved graham shard into the caramel on each for a fresh, just-placed look. Chill briefly to set the drizzle. (Caramel = SF syrup for 0 cal by default; see the note to swap in real brown-sugar caramel.)

payload.text: Break the graham into big shards (rough rectangles/quarters), not crumbs, and press them onto the top of the cookies BEFORE baking so they anchor, toast lightly, and stay crisp — folding crumbs into the dough buries them and they go soft. Reserve a couple of shards to set into the caramel after baking for a fresh, bakery look. SF caramel syrup is the lean 0-cal drizzle; the real brown-sugar caramel sub-recipe reads richer but costs calories.

payload.instructionText: Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough (if still tacky, chill 5 more minutes). Then cover and chill the dough ~30 minutes — same as the browned-butter cookies: a proper chill firms it for cleaner shaping and a thicker, chewier bake.

Preheat oven/air fryer to 330°F. Divide the chilled dough into 4 equal balls on parchment and press firmly with your palm or a glass to your target thickness — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread, so keep them on the thicker side for a chewy center rather than thin and dry. Then break the graham crackers into big rough shards (rectangles/quarters, not crumbs) and press 1-2 onto the top of each cookie, pushing them in slightly so they anchor and bake in — surface graham toasts lightly and stays crisp instead of going soft in the dough. Reserve a couple of shards for the finish.
